sentences.sentence: When Peter Osbeck. a Swedish priest, stopped off at the mid-Atlantic island of Ascension in 1752 on his way home from China, he wrote of 'a heap of ruinous rocks' with a bare, white mountain in the middle.
sentences.translation: 1752年，瑞典牧师彼得·奥斯贝克从中国返乡途中在大西洋中部的阿森松岛停留，他把这里描述为“一堆破败的岩石”，中央矗立着一座光秃秃的白色山峰。

structure.text: When Peter Osbeck. a Swedish priest, stopped off at the mid-Atlantic island of Ascension in 1752 on his way home from China
he wrote of 'a heap of ruinous rocks'
with a bare, white mountain in the middle

difficult_points: When 从句交代访问背景，主句 he 指 Peter Osbeck。
源文 Osbeck 后句点疑为逗号；a Swedish priest 是同位语，英文保持原样。

sentences.sentence: All it boasted was a couple of dozen species of plant, most of them ferns and some of them unique to the island.
sentences.translation: 它所拥有的不过二十来种植物，其中大多是蕨类，还有一些是该岛独有的物种。

structure.text: All it boasted
was a couple of dozen species of plant
most of them ferns and some of them unique to the island

difficult_points: All it boasted 相当于 All that it boasted，整体作主语。
末尾由两个省略 being 的独立主格结构说明植物构成。

sentences.sentence: And so it might have remained.
sentences.translation: 而它原本或许会一直维持这种状态。

structure.text: it
might have remained
so

difficult_points: it 指阿森松岛，so 指上段所述荒凉贫瘠的状态。
might have remained 表示与后来事实相反的过去可能性。

sentences.sentence: But in 1843 British plant collector Joseph Hooker made a brief call on his return from Antarctica.
sentences.translation: 但1843年，英国植物采集家约瑟夫·胡克从南极洲返程时曾在岛上短暂停留。

structure.text: British plant collector Joseph Hooker
made a brief call on his return from Antarctica

difficult_points: Joseph Hooker 与 British plant collector 是同位关系。
on his return from... 表示“从……返程时”。

sentences.sentence: Surveying the bare earth, he concluded that the island had suffered some natural calamity that had denuded it of vegetation and triggered a decline in rainfall that was turning the place into a desert.
sentences.translation: 考察这片裸露土地后，他断定该岛曾遭受某种自然灾害；灾害剥夺了岛上的植被，并导致降雨减少，使这里逐渐变成沙漠。

structure.text: Surveying the bare earth
he concluded
that the island had suffered some natural calamity
that had denuded it of vegetation and triggered a decline in rainfall
that was turning the place into a desert

difficult_points: 句首分词短语的逻辑主语是 he，即 Hooker。
第一个关系从句修饰 calamity，第二个修饰 decline in rainfall；it 指 island。

sentences.sentence: The British Navy, which by then maintained a garrison on the island, was keen to improve the place and asked Hooker's advice.
sentences.translation: 当时已在岛上驻军的英国海军很想改善这里的环境，于是向胡克征求意见。

structure.text: The British Navy
which by then maintained a garrison on the island
was keen to improve the place and asked Hooker's advice

difficult_points: which 从句补充说明英国海军当时与该岛的关系。
was keen 与 asked 是同一主语下的并列谓语，asked advice 意为征求意见。

sentences.sentence: He suggested an ambitious scheme for planting trees and shrubs that would revive rainfall and stimulate a wider ecological recovery.
sentences.translation: 他提出了一项雄心勃勃的植树种灌木计划，希望借此恢复降雨并推动更广泛的生态复苏。

structure.text: He suggested an ambitious scheme for planting trees and shrubs
that would revive rainfall and stimulate a wider ecological recovery

difficult_points: He 指 Hooker；for planting... 修饰 scheme。
that 从句修饰 scheme，would revive 与 stimulate 是并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: And, perhaps lacking anything else to do, the sailors set to with a will.
sentences.translation: 而水手们或许也无事可做，于是干劲十足地投入了这项工作。

structure.text: perhaps lacking anything else to do
the sailors
set to with a will

difficult_points: lacking... 的逻辑主语是 the sailors，表示他们积极行动的背景。
set to 表示着手工作，with a will 表示积极卖力地。

sentences.sentence: In 1845, a naval transport ship from Argentina delivered a batch of seedlings.
sentences.translation: 1845年，一艘来自阿根廷的海军运输船运来了一批幼苗。

structure.text: a naval transport ship from Argentina
delivered a batch of seedlings

difficult_points: from Argentina 修饰 ship，说明船只来处。
a batch of 表示成批运来的数量单位。

sentences.sentence: In the following years, more than 200 species of plant arrived from South Africa, from England came 700 packets of seeds, including those of two species that especially liked the place: bamboo and prickly pear.
sentences.translation: 随后几年里，南非送来了两百多种植物，英国则送来七百包种子，其中包括两种特别适应当地环境的植物：竹子和仙人掌。

structure.text: more than 200 species of plant arrived from South Africa
from England came 700 packets of seeds
including those of two species that especially liked the place
bamboo and prickly pear

difficult_points: from England came... 是地点状语前置造成的完全倒装。
those 替代 seeds；that 修饰 two species，liked the place 指特别适应当地。

sentences.sentence: With sailors planting several thousand trees a year, the bare white mountain was soon cloaked in green and renamed Green Mountain, and by the early twentieth century the mountain's slopes were covered with a variety of trees and shrubs from all over the world.
sentences.translation: 随着水手每年种下数千棵树，光秃的白山很快披上绿装，并被改名为“绿山”；到20世纪初，山坡已覆盖着来自世界各地的多种树木和灌木。

structure.text: With sailors planting several thousand trees a year
the bare white mountain was soon cloaked in green and renamed Green Mountain
by the early twentieth century the mountain's slopes were covered with a variety of trees and shrubs from all over the world

difficult_points: With sailors planting... 是 with 复合结构，说明变化发生的伴随背景。
was cloaked 与 renamed 共用助动词 was；后半句 by... 与过去完成状态 were covered 搭配。

sentences.sentence: Modern ecologists throw up their hands in horror at what they see as Hookers environmental anarchy.
sentences.translation: 现代生态学家对他们眼中胡克造成的环境混乱惊恐不已。

structure.text: Modern ecologists
throw up their hands in horror
at what they see as Hookers environmental anarchy

difficult_points: what 引导融合关系从句，作介词 at 的宾语。
see A as B 表示“把A视为B”；源文 Hookers 缺少所有格撇号，保持原样。

expressions.text: throw up one's hands in horror

sentences.sentence: The exotic species wrecked the indigenous ecosystem, squeezing out the islands endemic plants.
sentences.translation: 外来物种破坏了本土生态系统，并排挤了岛上的特有植物。

structure.text: The exotic species wrecked the indigenous ecosystem
squeezing out the islands endemic plants

difficult_points: squeezing out... 表示破坏生态系统造成的结果。
源文 islands 疑缺所有格撇号，语义为 the island's endemic plants。

sentences.sentence: In fact. Hooker knew well enough what might happen.
sentences.translation: 事实上，胡克很清楚可能会发生什么。

structure.text: In fact
Hooker knew well enough
what might happen

difficult_points: 源文 In fact 后使用句点，语义上仍是句首连接语，英文原样保留。
what might happen 是融合疑问从句，作 knew 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: However, he saw greater benefit in improving rainfall and encouraging more prolific vegetation on the island.
sentences.translation: 然而，他认为改善降雨、促进岛上植被繁茂会带来更大益处。

structure.text: However
he saw greater benefit in
improving rainfall and encouraging more prolific vegetation on the island

difficult_points: he 指 Hooker；However 将其预见风险与权衡后的选择对照。
improving 与 encouraging 是介词 in 后的并列动名词。

sentences.sentence: But there is a much deeper issue here than the relative benefits of sparse endemic species versus luxuriant imported ones.
sentences.translation: 但这里还有一个远比比较稀少本土物种与繁茂外来物种各自益处更深层的问题。

structure.text: there is a much deeper issue here
than the relative benefits of sparse endemic species versus luxuriant imported ones

difficult_points: than 引出 deeper 的比较对象，而不是另一个完整从句。
ones 替代 species；versus 表示两类物种的对照。

sentences.sentence: And as botanist David Wilkinson of Liverpool John Moores University in the UK pointed out after a recent visit to the island, it goes to the heart of some of the most dearly held tenets of ecology.
sentences.translation: 英国利物浦约翰摩尔斯大学的植物学家戴维·威尔金森最近考察该岛后指出，这个问题触及了一些最受珍视的生态学信条的核心。

structure.text: as botanist David Wilkinson of Liverpool John Moores University in the UK pointed out after a recent visit to the island
it goes to the heart of some of the most dearly held tenets of ecology

difficult_points: as 从句表示“正如……指出的”，主句 it 指上一句的 deeper issue。
dearly held 是过去分词短语修饰 tenets，表示人们极为珍视。

sentences.sentence: Conservationists' understandable concern for the fate of Ascension's handful of unique species has, he says, blinded them to something quite astonishing the fact that the introduced species have been a roaring success.
sentences.translation: 他说，保护人士对阿森松岛少数特有物种命运的合理担忧，使他们忽视了一个惊人的事实：引入物种取得了巨大成功。

structure.text: Conservationists' understandable concern for the fate of Ascension's handful of unique species
has, he says, blinded them to something quite astonishing
he says
the fact that the introduced species have been a roaring success

difficult_points: 长主语中心是 concern；he says 是插入语。
the fact... 与 something quite astonishing 同位，源文两者间省略了冒号或破折号。

sentences.sentence: Today's Green Mountain, says Wilkinson, is 'a fully functioning man-made tropical cloud forest'that has grown from scratch from a ragbag of species collected more or less at random from all over the planet.
sentences.translation: 威尔金森说，如今的绿山是“一片功能完整的人造热带云雾林”，它由从世界各地或多或少随机收集来的杂乱物种，从零开始生长而成。

structure.text: Today's Green Mountain
says Wilkinson
is 'a fully functioning man-made tropical cloud forest'
that has grown from scratch from a ragbag of species collected more or less at random from all over the planet

difficult_points: that 从句修饰 tropical cloud forest；源文 forest' 与 that 之间缺少空格。
collected... 修饰 species；more or less at random 表示近乎随机。

sentences.sentence: But how could it have happened?
sentences.translation: 但这究竟是怎么发生的呢？

structure.text: how
could it have happened

difficult_points: it 指人造云雾林成功形成这一现象。
could have happened 用情态动词完成式，追问过去现象如何成为可能。

sentences.sentence: Conventional ecological theory says that complex ecosystems such as cloud forests can emerge only through evolutionary processes in which each organism develops in concert with others to fill particular niches.
sentences.translation: 传统生态学理论认为，云雾林等复杂生态系统只能通过进化过程产生；在这一过程中，每种生物都与其他生物协同发展，以填补特定生态位。

structure.text: Conventional ecological theory says
that complex ecosystems such as cloud forests can emerge only through evolutionary processes
in which each organism develops in concert with others to fill particular niches

difficult_points: that 从句承载理论内容；in which 修饰 evolutionary processes。
to fill... 表示协同发展的目的；niches 在生态学中指生态位。

sentences.sentence: Plants eo-evolve with their pollinators and seed dispersers, while microbes in the soil evolve to deal with the leaf litter.
sentences.translation: 植物与其传粉者和种子传播者共同进化，而土壤中的微生物则进化出处理落叶层的能力。

structure.text: Plants eo-evolve with their pollinators and seed dispersers
while microbes in the soil evolve to deal with the leaf litter

difficult_points: while 在此并列对照两类共同进化过程。
源文 eo-evolve 疑为 co-evolve；英文保留原样，语义按“共同进化”理解。

sentences.sentence: But that's not what happened on Green Mountain.
sentences.translation: 但绿山上发生的情况并非如此。

structure.text: that's not
what happened on Green Mountain

difficult_points: that 指传统理论描述的共同进化过程。
what 引导融合关系从句，作表语，意为“绿山上发生的事情”。

sentences.sentence: And the experience suggests that perhaps natural rainforests are constructed far more by chance than by evolution.
sentences.translation: 而这段经历表明，天然雨林的形成或许更多依靠偶然，而非进化。

structure.text: the experience suggests
that perhaps natural rainforests are constructed far more by chance than by evolution

difficult_points: the experience 指绿山人造雨林形成的经历。
far more by chance than by evolution 是比较结构，强调偶然因素更大。

sentences.sentence: Species, say some ecologists, don't so much evolve to create ecosystems as make the best of what they have.
sentences.translation: 一些生态学家认为，物种与其说是通过进化去创造生态系统，不如说是在现有条件下尽力适应。

structure.text: Species
say some ecologists
don't so much evolve to create ecosystems as make the best of what they have
what they have

difficult_points: not so much A as B 表示“与其说A，不如说B”。
what they have 是 make the best of 的宾语，指物种面对的现有条件。

sentences.sentence: 'The Green Mountain system is a man-made system that has produced a tropical rainforest without any co-evolution between its constituent species,' says Wilkinson.
sentences.translation: 威尔金森说：“绿山系统是一个人造系统，它在组成该系统的物种之间没有任何共同进化的情况下，造就了一片热带雨林。”

structure.text: The Green Mountain system is a man-made system
that has produced a tropical rainforest without any co-evolution between its constituent species
says Wilkinson

difficult_points: that 修饰 a man-made system，说明该系统产生的结果。
its constituent species 指组成绿山系统的各物种。

sentences.sentence: Not everyone agrees.
sentences.translation: 并非所有人都同意这一观点。

structure.text: Not everyone
agrees

difficult_points: not everyone 是部分否定，意为“并非所有人”，不是“所有人都不”。
省略了 agrees with this view，观点由上文提供。

sentences.sentence: Alan Gray, an ecologist at the University of Edinburgh in the UK. argues that the surviving endemic species on Green Mountain, though small in number, may still form the framework of the new' ecosystem.
sentences.translation: 英国爱丁堡大学生态学家艾伦·格雷认为，绿山上幸存的特有物种尽管数量很少，却仍可能构成新生态系统的框架。

structure.text: Alan Gray, an ecologist at the University of Edinburgh in the UK. argues
that the surviving endemic species on Green Mountain, though small in number, may still form the framework of the new' ecosystem
though small in number

difficult_points: an ecologist... 是 Alan Gray 的同位语；源文 UK 后误用句点，英文保留。
though small in number 省略了 they are；源文 new 后多出撇号。

sentences.sentence: The new arrivals may just be an adornment, with little structural importance for the ecosystem.
sentences.translation: 新来的物种或许只是装饰，对生态系统的结构几乎没有重要作用。

structure.text: The new arrivals may just be an adornment
with little structural importance for the ecosystem

difficult_points: The new arrivals 指被引入的外来物种。
with... 补充说明这些物种在生态结构中的作用很小。

sentences.sentence: But to Wilkinson this sounds like clutching at straws.
sentences.translation: 但在威尔金森听来，这种说法不过是在抓救命稻草。

structure.text: to Wilkinson
this
sounds like clutching at straws

difficult_points: this 指格雷关于本土物种构成框架的解释。
clutching at straws 是习语，指绝望中抓住薄弱依据。

sentences.sentence: And the idea of the instant formation of rainforests sounds increasingly plausible as research reveals that supposedly pristine tropical rainforests from the Amazon to south-east Asia may in places be little more titan the overgrown gardens of past rainforest civilisations.
sentences.translation: 而随着研究揭示，从亚马孙到东南亚，那些据称原始未受扰动的热带雨林，在某些地方或许不过是古代雨林文明废弃后疯长的花园，雨林迅速形成的观点也显得越来越可信。

structure.text: the idea of the instant formation of rainforests sounds increasingly plausible
as research reveals
that supposedly pristine tropical rainforests from the Amazon to south-east Asia may in places be little more titan the overgrown gardens of past rainforest civilisations

difficult_points: as 引导原因从句，that 从句是 reveals 的宾语。
supposedly 表示“据称”，对 pristine 保留质疑；源文 titan 疑为 than，英文保留。

sentences.sentence: The most surprising thing of all is that no ecologists have thought to conduct proper research into this human-made rainforest ecosystem.
sentences.translation: 最令人惊讶的是，竟没有生态学家想到要对这个人造雨林生态系统开展正规的研究。

structure.text: The most surprising thing of all
is
that no ecologists have thought to conduct proper research into this human-made rainforest ecosystem

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，说明最令人惊讶的具体事实。
think to do 表示想到要做；research into 表示对某事进行研究。

sentences.sentence: A survey of the island's flora conducted six years ago by the University of Edinburgh was concerned only with endemic species.
sentences.translation: 爱丁堡大学六年前开展的一项岛上植物群调查，只关注特有物种。

structure.text: A survey of the island's flora conducted six years ago by the University of Edinburgh
conducted six years ago by the University of Edinburgh
was concerned only with endemic species

difficult_points: 长主语中心是 A survey，conducted... 后置修饰 survey。
be concerned with 在此表示研究“关注、涉及”某对象。

sentences.sentence: They characterised everything else as a threat.
sentences.translation: 他们把其他一切物种都定性为威胁。

structure.text: They
characterised
everything else
as a threat

difficult_points: They 指负责爱丁堡大学调查的研究者。
characterise A as B 表示把A描述或定性为B。

sentences.sentence: And the Ascension authorities are currently turning Green Mountain into a national park where introduced species, at least the invasive ones, are earmarked for culling rather than conservation.
sentences.translation: 阿森松岛当局目前正把绿山改建成国家公园；在那里，引入物种——至少其中的入侵物种——被列为清除对象，而非保护对象。

structure.text: the Ascension authorities are currently turning Green Mountain into a national park
where introduced species, at least the invasive ones, are earmarked for culling rather than conservation

difficult_points: where 修饰 a national park，说明园内对外来物种的处理。
at least the invasive ones 是插入语；be earmarked for 表示被指定用于某种处理。

sentences.sentence: Conservationists have understandable concerns, Wilkinson says.
sentences.translation: 威尔金森说，保护人士的担忧可以理解。

structure.text: Conservationists have understandable concerns
Wilkinson says

difficult_points: Wilkinson says 是后置报道语。
understandable 修饰 concerns，承认保护人士的立场具有合理性。

sentences.sentence: At least four endemic species have gone extinct on Ascension since the exotics started arriving.
sentences.translation: 自外来物种开始进入阿森松岛以来，至少已有四种特有物种灭绝。

structure.text: At least four endemic species have gone extinct on Ascension
since the exotics started arriving

difficult_points: since 引导时间从句，主句用现在完成时表示截至现在的结果。
the exotics 指前文引进的外来物种。

sentences.sentence: But in their urgency to protect endemics, ecologists are missing out on the study of a great enigma.
sentences.translation: 但生态学家急于保护特有物种，因而错失了研究一个巨大谜团的机会。

structure.text: in their urgency to protect endemics
ecologists are missing out on the study of a great enigma

difficult_points: in their urgency... 说明生态学家错失研究机会的原因。
endemics 名词化，指特有物种；miss out on 指未能获得机会。

sentences.sentence: 'As you walk through the forest, you see lots of leaves that have had chunks taken out of them by various insects.
sentences.translation: “当你穿行林中时，会看到许多叶片被各种昆虫咬掉了一块又一块。

structure.text: As you walk through the forest
you see lots of leaves
that have had chunks taken out of them by various insects

difficult_points: As 引导时间从句；you 是泛指观察者。
that 修饰 leaves；have chunks taken out 是 have + 宾语 + 过去分词结构。

sentences.sentence: There are caterpillars and beetles around.' says Wilkinson.
sentences.translation: 周围还能看到毛毛虫和甲虫。”威尔金森说。

structure.text: There are caterpillars and beetles around
says Wilkinson

difficult_points: there are 是存在句，around 表示在周围可见。
源文引号后以句点连接 says Wilkinson，英文原样保留。

sentences.sentence: 'But where did they come from?
sentences.translation: “但它们从哪里来？

structure.text: where
did they come from

difficult_points: they 指上句中的毛毛虫和甲虫。
where 前置后，介词 from 保留在句末。

sentences.sentence: Are they endemic or alien?
sentences.translation: 它们是本地物种还是外来物种？

structure.text: Are
they
endemic or alien

difficult_points: they 继续指这些昆虫。
endemic 与 alien 是并列表语，alien 在生态语境中指外来物种。

sentences.sentence: If alien, did they come with the plant on which they feed or discover it on arrival?'
sentences.translation: 如果是外来的，它们是随取食的植物一同到来，还是抵达后才发现这种植物的？”

structure.text: If alien
did they come with the plant on which they feed
or discover it on arrival
on which they feed

difficult_points: If alien 省略了 they are；did 同时支配 come 与 discover。
on which they feed 修饰 plant，feed on 的介词被前置；it 指该植物。

sentences.sentence: Such questions go to the heart of how- rainforests happen.
sentences.translation: 这类问题触及雨林究竟如何形成这一核心。

structure.text: Such questions
go to the heart of
how- rainforests happen

difficult_points: how 从句作介词 of 的宾语，探讨雨林形成机制。
源文 how 后多出连字符，英文原样保留。

sentences.sentence: The Green Mountain forest holds many secrets.
sentences.translation: 绿山森林隐藏着许多秘密。

structure.text: The Green Mountain forest
holds many secrets

difficult_points: holds 在此并非字面“拿着”，而是指包含、隐藏。
many secrets 指物种来源与生态系统形成机制等未解问题。

sentences.sentence: And the irony is that the most artificial rainforest in the world could tell us more about rainforest ecology than any number of natural forests.
sentences.translation: 而讽刺的是，世界上最人工化的雨林，可能比无论多少片天然森林都更能帮助我们理解雨林生态。

structure.text: the irony is
that the most artificial rainforest in the world could tell us more about rainforest ecology than any number of natural forests

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，解释 irony 的具体内容。
tell us more about... 是拟人用法；any number of 表示再多的天然森林也无法相比。
